violations.text: Tartar sauce which was prepared on site, date marked 11/19 was observed held beyond the 7 days. Food items exceeding the 7 days shall be discarded. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/08/2025

Beverage tubing was observed in contact with ice used for drinks at the bar. Beverage tubing and cold plate beverage cooling devices must not be in contact with stored ice used for drinks. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/08/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed torn and in poor repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025

Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

Raw animal foods (shelled eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (mashed potatoes) in the prep unit. Raw animal foods (shrimp) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (vegetables) in the reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

Tartar sauce was observed held at 60 degrees Fahrenheit on top of prep unit. Per interview with PIC, sauce was used less than 1 hour prior. Sauce was moved to prep unit at time of inspection. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. At time of inspection, mashed potatoes were placed hot in the refrigerator in COVERED containers. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

The door to the outside, located in the dining area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025
Bottled beverages were observed stored in ice that was being used for drinks. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the basement storage area. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025
The following door gaskets were observed torn and in poor repair: 1) Refrigerator #1 2) Prep Unit #2 New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the fan have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025

The grease refuse outside is located on dirt. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. New Violation. Correct By: 01/01/2026

Shellstock (clams) located in the walk-in cooler did not have the proper identification tags. Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the shellstock are received until the container is empty. The tag was placed with the clams during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024

Reduced oxygen packaged (ROP) seafood (tuna) was observed thawing with the package intact. ROP seafood must be completely removed from the package when thawing. Tuna was observed held at 37 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Tuna was removed from the package during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024

Menu is lacking a food allergy statement notifying customers of their obligation to notify staff if they have a food allergy. Per person in charge, a new menu will be printed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2023

The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Written procedures were given to the person in charge at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2023
The hand-washing sink in the bar was lacking paper towels. An employee placed paper towels at the bar at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2023

A strainer was observed in the hand-washing sink at the bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Strainer was relocated at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2023

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 10 parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. A new chlorine sanitizing solution was mixed during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. The can opener was washed during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2023

Burgers are served raw or undercooked to the customer's request. A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. Per person in charge, a new menu will be printed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2023

Tuna, beef, and guacamole were observed thawing in the prep sink. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Food was relocated to a cold-hold unit during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2023

Reduced oxygen packaged (ROP) seafood (lobster and tuna), labeled keep frozen, were observed thawed with the package intact. ROP seafood must be removed from the package when thawing. Tuna was observed held at 46-49 degrees Fahrenheit and lobster was observed held at 46 degrees Fahrenheit. Tuna and lobster were voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2023
